name: Gin (High-Performance Go Web Framework)

auth.notes: Web framework; integrate JWT/session auth via middleware

performance.notes: httprouter-based; one of the fastest Go frameworks; ~40x faster than Martini

agent_readiness.notes: Most popular Go web framework. gin.New() / gin.Default(). c.JSON(200, gin.H{"key": val}). c.ShouldBindJSON(&obj) for request binding. c.Param('id') for URL params. Middleware: r.Use(middleware). Groups: r.Group('/api/v1'). Validation via validator tags. HTML templates. Multipart file upload. SSE. CORS middleware. Massive ecosystem and community. Used by Docker, Kubernetes tooling, countless Go APIs. Higher level than chi; lower level than Ruby on Rails. The Express.js of Go.

gotchas: Context (gin.Context) not compatible with net/http middleware ecosystem
Validation errors can be verbose to handle
Default gin.Default() includes Logger+Recover but logs everything
Error handling conventions less clear than Echo
best_when: Teams wanting most popular Go framework with large ecosystem
Projects needing built-in JSON binding, validation, middleware
Standard CRUD APIs, webhooks, web services in Go
avoid_when: Teams preferring idiomatic Go over framework patterns
summary: Gin is the most popular Go web framework. c.JSON(), c.ShouldBindJSON(), URL params, middleware groups. Built on httprouter for high performance. Massive ecosystem and community. Built-in validation via struct tags. The Express.js equivalent for Go. Best for REST APIs wanting batteries-included. chi for net/http compatibility; Echo for cleaner error handling; Fiber for maximum performance.
